points: Materials are substances used to make objects; chosen based on properties and the intended purpose.
Classification is the method of grouping objects based on common features to find patterns and items easily.
Indian history (Harappan era) shows advanced pottery techniques using terracotta (baked clay).
Appearance: Materials can be lustrous (metals like gold, iron) or non-lustrous (wood, paper).
Hardness: Hard materials (stone, iron) are harder to scratch than soft materials (sponge, rubber).
Transparency: Materials are categorized as transparent (glass), opaque (wood), or translucent (frosted glass) depending on how clearly we can see through them.
Solubility: Substances that disappear in water are soluble (salt, sugar); others are insoluble (sand, sawdust).
Matter is anything that has mass (amount of material) and volume (space occupied).
Standard units include kilogram (kg) for mass and cubic metre (m³) or litre (L) for volume.
Ancient Ayurveda classifies matter using 20 opposite properties (gunas), like heavy/light and cold/hot.
